---
title: "Publications"
body-classes: "publications-page"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right          # Right margin (default, but explicit)
    toc-title: "On this page"    # Matches your screenshot/circle
    toc-depth: 2                 # Shows ## headings (major sections)
    toc-expand: true             # Fully expanded by default for quick scan
    css: styles.css              # Keep if you have custom styles
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.5.0/css/all.min.css">
---
Here you will find my peer-reviewed journal articles, manuscripts under review, conference proceedings, and presentations. My work focuses on the epidemiology of infectious diseases in dairy cattle in Bangladesh, with emphasis on Bayesian methods, prevalence estimation, risk factor analysis, and zoonotic threats (brucellosis, paratuberculosis, BVD, Coxiella, etc.).

I have contributed significantly as first or equally contributing first author in most entries, leading study design, advanced statistical modeling (Bayesian latent-class, hierarchical, mixture models, multivariable/multinomial regression), field diagnostics, and visualization.

## Published Journal Articles {.published}

<i class="fa-solid fa-check-circle" style="color: #28a745;"></i> **Published**

- Kibria, A.M., **Islam, M.N.**, Alam, M.S., Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Rafeh, A.A., Hasan, R.S.M., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  True prevalence and associated risk factors of bovine paratuberculosis antibodies in dairy herds in Bangladesh. *PLOS ONE*.  
  [https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0334683](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0334683)  
  **Equally contributing first author** — Contributed to study design, methodology development, Bayesian latent-class prevalence estimation, data analysis, visualization, and manuscript preparation.

- Mahmud Sajeeb, M.S., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Islam, M.M., Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Prevalence and risk factors of bovine viral diarrhea virus antibodies in dairy herds of Bangladesh. *Veterinary Sciences*, 12(8), 739.  
  [https://doi.org/10.3390/vetsci12080739](https://doi.org/10.3390/vetsci12080739)  
  **Equally contributing first author** — Study design, field sampling, ELISA diagnostics, multivariable regression analysis, visualization, and manuscript preparation.

## Manuscripts Under Review {.under-review}

<i class="fa-solid fa-hourglass-half" style="color: #fd7e14;"></i> **Under Review**

- **Islam, M.N.** (first author), Haque, M.Z., Alam, M.S., Islam, S., Adhikari, B.J., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Bayesian estimation of the true prevalence of *Coxiella burnetii* milk antibodies and associated risk factors in dairy herds in Bangladesh. *Veterinary Medicine and Science*.

- Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Mahmud, R.S., Rahman, M.S., Ariful Islam, M., Aktaruzzaman, M., Meletis, L., Kostoulas, P., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Bayesian hierarchical mixture modelling to derive probabilistic iELISA thresholds for bovine brucellosis in Bangladeshi dairy herds. *Animal Diseases*.

- Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Mahmud, R.S., Rahman, M.S., Ariful Islam, M., Aktaruzzaman, M., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Risk factors for latent and active brucellosis in dairy cattle: a Bayesian mixed-effects multinomial regression analysis in large-scale Bangladeshi herds. *Epidemiology & Infection*.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., Ariful Islam, M., Shanta, S.A., Tahomina Akter, M., Hossain, M.T., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Meletis, L., Kostoulas, P., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Bayesian Gaussian mixture modeling to determine somatic cell count thresholds for subclinical mastitis diagnosis in dairy cows. *PLOS ONE*.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., M. Tahomina Akter, Mazhar Islam, M., Ariful Islam, M., Hossain, M.T., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Economic impact of subclinical mastitis in Bangladeshi crossbred dairy cattle. *Preventive Veterinary Medicine*.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., Ariful Islam, M., Shanta, S.A., Tahomina Akter, M., Hossain, M.T., **Islam, M.N.**, Alam, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Prevalence and cow-level risk factors for mild and severe subclinical mastitis in intensive and extensive dairy systems in Sirajganj, Bangladesh. *Veterinary Medicine International*.

- Saha, U.K., **Islam, M.N.** (equally contributing first author), Chaudhary, P., Hassan Alif, M.K., Mim, S.N., & Rahman, M.S. (2026).  
  Exploratory longitudinal ELISA antibody responses and hematological parameters after heat-inactivated *Brucella abortus* vaccination in cattle. *Acta Veterinaria Hungarica*.

- Saif, M.A., **Islam, M.N.**, Ferdous, M.J., Rahman, A.K.M.A., & Bari, F.Y. (2026).  
  Early-life feeding and management interventions on lamb survivability. *Bulgarian Journal of Veterinary Medicine*.

- Saif, M.A., **Islam, M.N.**, Ferdous, M.J., Rahman, A.K.M.A., & Bari, F.Y. (2026).  
  Integrated immune and protein profiles predict growth in preweaning lambs independent of feeding regimen and maternal oxytocin administration. *Iraqi Journal of Veterinary Sciences*.

## Conference Proceedings & Papers

- **Islam, M.**; Alam, M.; Islam, S.; Adhikari, B.; Rahman, A. (2025).  
  Feasibility of on-farm somatic cell count screening for subclinical mastitis: Prevalence and risk factors in a commercial dairy herd in Bangladesh. In *Proceedings of the 1st International Online Conference on Veterinary Sciences*, 3–5 December 2025, MDPI: Basel, Switzerland.  
  [https://sciforum.net/paper/view/27422](https://sciforum.net/paper/view/27422)

- Alam, M. S., **Islam, M. N.**, Rafeh, A. A., Adhikari, B. J., Kibria, M. A., Mahmud, M. S., Ema, A. A., Islam, S., Rahman, M. S., Rahman, A. K. M. A. (2025).  
  Herd-level prevalence of brucellosis, paratuberculosis, neosporosis, and bovine viral diarrhea in large dairy herds in Bangladesh. *BAU Research Progress*, 35.

- Alam, M. S., **Islam, M. N.**, Rafeh, A. A., Rahman, M. S., Islam, M. A., & Rahman, A. K. M. (2024).  
  Herd-level prevalence of brucellosis in large dairy herds in Bangladesh. *Proceedings of the BSVER Annual Scientific Conference 30*, Bangladesh Agricultural University, Mymensingh, February 24-25, 2024.

## Conference Presentations & Posters

- **Feasibility of On-Farm Somatic Cell Count Screening for Subclinical Mastitis: Prevalence and Risk Factors in a Commercial Dairy Herd in Bangladesh.**  
  Presented at IOCVS 2025, 4 December 2025. [DOI: 10.13140/RG.2.2.16208.16642](https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.16208.16642).

- **Prevalence and Epidemiology of Bovine Brucellosis in Large Dairy Farms of Bangladesh.**  
  Research poster on prevalence, risk factors, and control strategies presented at the BSVER Annual Scientific Conference 2024, Bangladesh Agricultural University, Mymensingh, February 24-25, 2024.
